text.title: SERP Movement Alert Rules Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `SERP Movement Alert Rules` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Trigger alert when week-over-week impressions for a keyword (or query group) drop >= 30%. Compare against 4-week rolling average to filter noise.

rationale.source: GSC alerting practice; Search Engine Land 2024

rule.rationale: 30% WoW drop is outside normal seasonal noise; comparable to a manual penalty or core update hit. Smaller thresholds produce too many false positives.

rule.statement: Trigger alert when a tracked keyword's average position degrades by >= 3 ranks in a 7-day window. Rank-tracker daily data; smoothing via 7-day average.
rule.rationale: 3-rank drop on a top-10 keyword is loss of 50%+ click share. Smaller drops are within normal SERP volatility; bigger thresholds miss early signal.

rule.statement: Trigger alert any time an AI Overview (SGE / AIO) starts appearing on a tracked keyword's SERP. Detection via rank-tracker SERP feature flag or scraping.
rule.rationale: AI Overview typically halves click-through for the first organic result. Late detection = weeks of CTR collapse before team notices.

rule.statement: Trigger alert when CTR for a query drops > 50% relative to its 4-week rolling average AND impressions stable. Filters out genuine traffic drops.
rule.rationale: Stable impressions + collapsing CTR means SERP layout changed (AI Overview, featured snippet steal, ad block expansion). Different fix than ranking drop.

rule.statement: Every alert MUST have a documented 48-hour review SLA with a named owner. Alerts without owner are ignored within weeks.

rationale.source: Internal SRE alerting discipline applied to SEO

rule.rationale: Unowned alerts = noise. Named owner + SLA forces triage decision: real → investigate, noise → adjust threshold.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
